# 🏋️**SERVER C O R E TRAINING PROGRAM**

## ℹ️Introduction

---

### 👋**Welcome to the Server Training Program**

**![star2 emoji][image1]** **Introduction**

Welcome to the Server Training Program for Three Points Hospitality\! Over the next five days, you will be equipped with the skills and knowledge needed to excel in your role as a server at one of our establishments. We’re excited to have you on board and look forward to seeing you thrive\!

### 🎯**Purpose & Targets**

## **![white\_check\_mark emoji][image2] Purpose**

During your training period, use this program as a reference for each shift. You have 10 days from your first training shift to complete and pass your test, which covers the material outlined below. You can take your test as early as day one of training if you've studied and feel comfortable with all the material. You'll be expected to demonstrate sufficient understanding to perform server duties efficiently alongside your co-workers. Successfully completing this test within the 10-day window is required before you'll be placed on the regular schedule and eligible to earn tips.

---

## ![dart emoji][image3] **Targets**

**When you complete this training, you will be able to:**

* Take and deliver orders accurately and efficiently.  
* Provide exceptional customer service, ensuring guest satisfaction.  
* Demonstrate comprehensive knowledge of the menu, including ingredients and preparation methods.  
* Handle payments and operate the POS system proficiently.  
* Maintain a clean and organized dining area.

---

## ![memo emoji][image4] **Overview**

This training will teach you all about the role of a server at Three Points Hospitality, what you need to know, and how to perform your duties effectively. You'll learn about our team, the essential procedures in the Server Manual, and the importance of delivering outstanding customer service. You'll also gain expertise in taking orders, managing the dining area, and understanding our menu in detail. All of this will equip you with the best possible start as the newest member of our serving team.

### 💯**Required Testing**

### **Testing will include:**

* **![book emoji][image5]** Server Manual  
* ![shield emoji][image6] Responsible Alcohol Service  
* ![plate\_with\_cutlery emoji][image7] Food Menu  
* ![pancakes emoji][image8] Brunch Food Menu {location based}  
* ![cocktail emoji][image9] Dinner Cocktails  
* ![tropical\_drink emoji][image10] Alcohol Knowledge

![fire emoji][image11] **Pro Tip:** After setting up the necessary resources and knowledge base, it is crucial to practice and refine the skills required for serving. Hands-on experience in taking and delivering orders, mastering the art of customer service, and understanding the menu are essential aspects of becoming a successful server. Participating in ongoing trainings and working in different dining settings can greatly contribute to honing these skills. Networking with other professionals in the field and exchanging ideas can also provide valuable insights and inspiration for enhancing the dining experience.

### ✴️**Day Training Schedules**

The C.O.R.E. Training Program offers a structured daily training schedule tailored to each position across all our locations, ensuring consistency and thoroughness in your learning experience. Throughout your training, you will engage in hands-on activities, role-specific tasks, and guided learning sessions, all aimed at preparing you to deliver exceptional service. Whether you are training as a server, bartender, host, or any other key position, this program will provide you with the tools necessary to succeed and grow in your role.

## **Please scroll to the venue you will be working at to view the**

## **daily training schedule pertaining to your role.**

## 1️⃣Server Day 1: Laying the Groundwork

---

### 🎯**Objectives**

## **What You'll Learn Today**

Welcome to your first day of training\! You'll begin with an introduction to the company, expectations of the server role, and foundational service skills. You'll review key policies, familiarize yourself with the restaurant layout, and observe how experienced servers interact with guests.

## **Why It's Important**

A great server is attentive, efficient, and knowledgeable. Learning service flow, guest interaction skills, and menu knowledge will help you deliver exceptional experiences from day one. Today's foundation will set you up for high-volume service and advanced techniques in the days ahead.

## 

### ✅**📋 Training Checklist & Key Points**

## **Step 1: Welcome & Introduction**

* **Meet with the Manager on Duty (MOD) to submit paperwork, clock in, and get acquainted with the venue.**  
  * *Your trainer will introduce you to the 5-day training program, explaining on-site and off-site expectations.*  
  * *Use this time to ask any questions about your training schedule or expectations.*

## **Step 2: Company Policies & Procedures**

* **Review the company mission, vision, and core values.**  
  * *Discuss attendance policies, uniform expectations, and the structure of the training program.*  
  * *Understanding our core values ensures consistency and professionalism in service.*

## **Step 3: Uniforms, Personal Hygiene, Health & Safety Regulations, Serving Alcohol**

* **Learn the importance of maintaining a professional appearance and following hygiene standards.**  
  * *Review the Alcohol Agreement & Training requirements for responsible service.*  
  * *Cleanliness and personal hygiene are key to guest trust and upholding safety standards.*

## **Step 4: Payrolls, Tips & Scheduling**

* **Your trainer will go over payroll systems, tip distribution, and scheduling tools (7Shifts & Branch).**  
  * *Understand how you're scheduled, how you're paid, and ways to optimize earnings.*  
  * *A well-prepared server can focus on service without payroll concerns.*

## **Step 5: The Grand Tour**

* **Your trainer will give you a tour of the venue, including:**  
  * *Front-of-House (FOH): Dining sections, POS stations, host stand, bar, restrooms, entryways, and emergency exits.*  
  * *Back-of-House (BOH): Service stations, kitchen areas, dish pit, walk-in coolers/freezers, ice machines, storage areas, and employee break areas.*  
  * *Knowing your surroundings ensures efficiency during shifts.*

## **Step 6: Opening Checklist**

* **Review and complete the opening checklist using the Jolt app with your trainer.**  
  * *Learn proper setup procedures for expo stations and drink stations.*  
  * *Observing and following the opening process prepares you for independent execution.*

## **Step 7: Basic Serving Skills**

* **Learn proper greeting techniques, table approach, and tray/plate carrying.**  
  * *Understand "full hands in, full hands out" for efficiency.*  
  * *Practice inputting orders into the Toast POS system.*

## **Step 8: Table Setting, Numbers & Seating**

* **Observe and practice setting tables to restaurant standards.**  
  * *Learn table and seat numbers to ensure precise service.*  
  * *Accurate knowledge of seating arrangements improves order efficiency.*

## **Step 9: Proper Service Etiquette in Clearing Tables**

* **Discuss polite table-clearing etiquette and the correct time to clear items.**  
  * *Review guest interactions regarding when to remove plates.*  
  * *A clean and reset table enhances the guest experience.*

## **Step 10: Guest Interaction**

* **Observe how your trainer greets tables and interacts with guests.**  
  * *Ensure your introduction is warm, unique, and welcoming.*  
  * *Learn how to mention specials and allow guests time to review menus.*

## **Step 11: Service Observation**

* **Watch your trainer manage their tables to understand pacing, guest requests, and upselling techniques.**  
  * *Take note of how drink refills and check-ins are timed to enhance service flow.*  
  * *Learning from an experienced server provides valuable insights into efficiency.*

## **Step 12: POS System Introduction**

* **Observe and learn the Toast POS system, including:**  
  * *Inputting orders, separating by seat number, and processing payments.*  
  * *Splitting checks, adjusting tips, and handling voids or modifications.*  
  * *Efficiency in the POS system ensures smooth service and accurate billing.*

## **Step 13: Menu Familiarization**

* **Review the food menu, including specials, core dishes, and drink options.**  
  * *Learn suggested food and beverage pairings.*  
  * *Understanding menu items allows for confident guest recommendations.*

## **Step 14: Common Modifications**

* **Discuss frequent menu modifications, including dietary restrictions and allergies.**  
  * *Review gluten-free, vegetarian, and vegan menu items.*  
  * *Knowing common modifications helps accommodate guest needs smoothly.*

## **Step 15: End-of-Shift Recap**

* **Review and reinforce the key concepts from the day's training.**  
  * *Reflect on what was learned and identify areas for further practice.*  
  * *Ensure readiness for Day 2 training by reviewing key points.*

# 

### ⭐**Enhancements: Activities & Demonstrations**

## **![star2 emoji][image12] Building the Basics of Service & Flow**

* **Venue Walkthrough:** Trainer-led tour focusing on guest areas, service stations, and key storage locations.  
* **Service Observation Drill:** Watch a senior server complete an entire table service cycle and take notes.  
* **Table Number Memory Challenge:** Quiz yourself on seat numbers and table layout for efficient service.

## ![bulb emoji][image13] **Pro Tips**

* **Stay Observant:** Pay close attention to experienced servers and their guest interactions.  
* **Ask Questions:** Use training time to clarify anything about menu items, service expectations, or venue logistics.  
* **First Impressions Matter:** A confident greeting sets the tone for great service—practice your table approach.

## ![white\_check\_mark emoji][image14] **Trainer to Trainee: Check for Understanding**

* **Comprehension Check:** "What are the key responsibilities of a server beyond taking orders?"  
* **Scenario:** "A guest has a food allergy and asks about menu options. How do you ensure their safety?"

## ![dart emoji][image15] **Performance Targets**

* **Company Knowledge:** Demonstrate understanding of venue expectations, policies, and service standards.  
* **Venue Familiarity:** Identify key locations in FOH and BOH to ensure smooth workflow.  
* **Basic Service Knowledge:** Display confidence in table numbers, basic serving techniques, and POS navigation.  
* **Guest Interaction Readiness:** Observe and understand best practices in greeting and table management.

### 🏁**Conclusion**

| By the end of Day 1, you should feel comfortable navigating the restaurant, familiar with company policies, and confident in observing service interactions. Today's training focused on laying the foundation for efficiency, guest engagement, and professionalism in service. |
| :---: |

### ![pushpin emoji][image18] **Next Steps**

* **Complete the DAY 1 Training Session & Knowledge Check before arriving for Day 2\.**  
* **Review the Server Handbook again to reinforce today's learning.**  
* **Start memorizing table numbers and menu offerings to improve speed and confidence.**

## 2️⃣Server Day 2: Strengthening Service Skills

---

### 🎯**Objectives**

## **What You'll Learn Today**

Building on what you learned in Day 1, today's focus is on deepening your menu knowledge, refining service techniques, and introducing upselling strategies. You'll also practice taking and inputting orders while learning how to manage multiple tables efficiently.

## **Why It's Important**

Guests rely on servers to provide accurate information about food, beverages, and modifications. Your confidence in the menu and service flow will directly impact guest satisfaction. Practicing today's skills will prepare you to handle guest requests, upsell effectively, and work efficiently during peak hours.

## **TIME TO SHOW THOSE SKILLS**

## 

### ✅**Training Checklist & Key Points**

## **Step 1: Opening Checklist**

* **Arrive promptly, clock in, and complete the opening checklist using the Jolt app.**  
  * *Work with your trainer to ensure all FOH areas are guest-ready.*  
  * *A properly set-up dining area leads to a smooth service from the start.*

## **Step 2: Service Sequence Review**

* **Walk through the entire service flow—from greeting to farewell.**  
  * *Practice approaching a table, taking orders, and presenting checks.*  
  * *Consistency in service ensures a seamless guest experience.*

## **Step 3: Trainer Support & Guest Engagement**

* **Begin practicing order-taking, answering guest questions, and handling special requests.**  
  * *Learn how to manage multiple tables efficiently.*  
  * *Guest interaction and attentiveness directly impact guest satisfaction and tips.*

## **Step 4: Menu Training**

* **Review key menu items, including ingredients and preparation methods.**  
  * *Discuss guest favorites, seasonal specials, and high-margin items.*  
  * *A deep understanding of the menu makes you a trusted resource for guests.*

## **Step 5: Review of Food Allergies**

* **Learn about common food allergies (nuts, dairy, gluten, shellfish, soy, eggs).**  
  * *Understand the severity of allergic reactions and how to prevent cross-contamination.*  
  * *Guest safety is the top priority when handling dietary restrictions.*

## **Step 6: Review of Dietary Options**

* **Understand different dietary restrictions (vegetarian, vegan, gluten-free, keto).**  
  * *Familiarize yourself with menu options that cater to these needs.*  
  * *Being prepared with substitutions makes service smoother and more enjoyable for guests.*

## **Step 7: Beverage Service Basics**

* **Review liquor, beer, wine, and signature cocktails.**  
  * *Understand responsible alcohol service and ID verification.*  
  * *Proper knowledge of beverages allows you to make great pairing suggestions.*

## **Step 8: Table Management & Pacing**

* **Learn techniques for managing multiple tables efficiently.**  
  * *Prioritize tasks, maintain table awareness, and stay organized.*  
  * *Balancing multiple guests ensures that service runs smoothly even during busy hours.*

## **Step 9: Teamwork & Communication**

* **Understand the importance of pre-shift meetings and ongoing team communication.**  
  * *Work closely with kitchen staff, bartenders, and other servers.*  
  * *Strong communication leads to fewer mistakes and better guest service.*

## **Step 10: Upselling Techniques**

* **Learn strategies for increasing check averages through effective suggestions.**  
  * *Practice upselling with a trainer using real menu scenarios.*  
  * *Recommending enhancements improves the guest experience and increases sales.*

## **Step 11: Service Skills Practice**

* **Continue working with your trainer to take orders, run food, and interact with guests.**  
  * *Refine service etiquette, pacing, and handling guest needs.*  
  * *Hands-on practice builds confidence in real-world service scenarios.*

## **Step 12: Toast POS Practice**

* **Practice advanced POS functions (split checks, managing tabs, special requests).**  
  * *Troubleshoot common POS issues with your trainer.*  
  * *Mastering the POS system ensures efficiency and accuracy during peak service.*

## **Step 13: End-of-Shift Recap**

* **Review today's key concepts with your trainer.**  
  * *Discuss what went well and areas for improvement.*  
  * *Reflecting on service helps refine skills for the next shift.*

# 

### ⭐**Enhancements: Activities & Demonstrations**

## **![chart\_with\_upwards\_trend emoji][image19] Strengthening Service Steps & Menu Mastery**

* **Table-Side Interaction Drill:** Roleplay taking orders with guests who have specific dietary needs or modifications.  
* **Upselling Scenarios:** Practice recommending add-ons, beverage pairings, and dessert options in live role-play.  
* **Service Pacing Test:** Work with a trainer to practice handling multiple tables, tracking order timing, and ensuring proper course pacing.

---

## ![bulb emoji][image20] **Pro Tips**

* **Anticipate Needs:** Always offer drink refills, extra napkins, or condiments before the guest asks.  
* **Read Your Guests:** Some guests prefer quick service, while others enjoy conversation—adjust your approach accordingly.  
* **Confidence is Key:** Guests trust servers who confidently describe dishes and recommend pairings.

---

## ![white\_check\_mark emoji][image21] **Trainer to Trainee: Check for Understanding**

* **Comprehension Check:** "What is the best way to handle a guest with a severe food allergy?"  
* **Scenario:** "A guest is hesitant about ordering a specific dish. How do you guide them to a confident decision?"

### 

---

## ![dart emoji][image22] **Performance Targets**

* **Menu Knowledge:** Demonstrate understanding of food ingredients, preparation, and dietary accommodations.  
* **Service Pacing:** Successfully manage multiple tables while maintaining efficiency.  
* **Upselling Skills:** Confidently recommend add-ons and pairings to enhance guest experience.  
* **POS Mastery:** Input orders and process checks without errors or delays.

## 3️⃣Server Day 3: Mastering Menu Knowledge & Complex Service Scenarios

---

### 🎯**Objectives**

## **What You'll Learn Today**

Today’s focus is on mastering menu knowledge, handling special requests, and navigating complex service scenarios. You'll engage in cross-training to understand how different roles contribute to smooth service. Additionally, you'll sharpen your ability to make recommendations, upsell effectively, and problem-solve guest concerns.

## **Why It’s Important**

A knowledgeable server anticipates guest needs, confidently explains the menu, and provides solutions in real time. Understanding menu items, dietary accommodations, and service strategies will enhance both the guest experience and your efficiency. By today’s end, you’ll be better equipped to handle guest concerns and work seamlessly with the team.

### ✅**Training Checklist & Key Points**

# **![clipboard emoji][image26] Training Checklist & Key Points**

## **Step 1: Independent Service Practice**

* **Begin working with your trainer while taking more responsibility for your tables.**  
  * *Manage tables with trainer oversight, while shadowing them for larger parties.*  
  * *Gain confidence in pacing service and prioritizing multiple guests.*

## **Step 2: Comprehensive Menu Training**

* **Deep dive into all menu items, including ingredients and preparation methods.**  
  * *Identify key allergens and dietary restrictions associated with each dish.*  
  * *Understand the origins of specialty dishes and how to describe them confidently.*

## **Step 3: Beverage Menu Deep Dive**

* **Review detailed knowledge of the wine list, beer selection, and signature cocktails.**  
  * *Discuss flavor profiles and ideal pairings with food menu items.*  
  * *Master basic beverage terminology to enhance guest recommendations.*

## **Step 4: Advanced Service Techniques**

* **Learn how to handle guest complaints and resolve service issues professionally.**  
  * *Practice maintaining composure and problem-solving under pressure.*  
  * *Understand how to offer solutions while following company policies.*

## **Step 5: Complex Service Scenarios**

* **Simulate handling difficult guest situations, including allergies, modifications, and high-stress environments.**  
  * *Role-play common service challenges and how to navigate them effectively.*  
  * *Learn how to involve management when necessary without escalating tension.*

## **Step 6: Handling Special Requests**

* **Discuss accommodating dietary restrictions and how to communicate modifications with the kitchen.**  
  * *Understand the difference between allergies and preferences to avoid miscommunication.*  
  * *Ensure cross-contamination prevention procedures are followed.*

| Expo Training Session \#1 Objective: Introduction to Expo Training and Demonstrate Readiness for Independent Work |
| ----- |

*This segment focuses on mastering the expo role by efficiently coordinating between the kitchen and dining area. You must demonstrate readiness to work independently and grasp expo responsibilities. By the end, you should be confident in front-of-house duties and understand seamless restaurant operations.*

## **Your Tasks:**

* **Expo Station Setup:** Work with the current expo or manager on duty throughout this training session. Review items stocked for the dinner shift and ensure everything is set up with backup items ready to go.  
* **Expediting Food Orders:** Expedite food tickets with a trainer or manager. Run food to tables according to printed tickets.  
* **Understanding the Expo Role:** Learn the responsibilities of the expo position, including organizing and coordinating food orders, communicating with kitchen staff, and ensuring order accuracy.  
* **Station Cleanliness:** Maintain station cleanliness throughout the shift using sanitizer solution, sanitizer wipes, gloves, and rags.  
* **Expo Station Setup Recap:** Review the proper setup of the expo station, including organizing tickets, garnishes, condiments, and necessary utensils.  
* **Order Coordination:** Practice coordinating orders between the kitchen and serving staff. Ensure each dish is prepared to correct specifications and all components of an order are complete before sending it out.  
* **Quality Control:** Understand the importance of quality control. Check each plate for presentation, temperature, and accuracy before it leaves the kitchen.  
* **Timing and Efficiency:** Learn techniques for managing the timing of orders, especially during peak service times. Ensure hot foods remain hot and cold foods remain cold.  
* **Communication Skills:** Enhance your communication skills to efficiently relay information between kitchen staff and servers. Practice clear and concise communication to avoid misunderstandings.  
* **Handling Special Requests:** Learn how to handle special requests and modifications efficiently. Ensure all dietary restrictions and allergies are communicated clearly to the kitchen staff.

| Once you've completed the Cross-Training Expo Session, please find your assigned trainer for the remainder of your shift. |
| :---- |

## **Step 8: Product Assessment Questionnaire**

* **Engage in a quiz with your trainer to test your knowledge of key menu items.**  
  * *Demonstrate your ability to describe dishes, allergens, and modifications confidently.*  
  * *Review guest FAQs and practice responding with clear, professional explanations.*

## **Step 9: Ending Shift Checklist**

* **Complete the mid/closing checklist using the Jolt app.**  
  * *Ensure tables are fully reset, service areas are stocked, and the restaurant is prepped for the next shift.*  
  * *Confirm that all tasks are completed before clocking out.*

## **Step 10: Closing Reports**

* **Run the server shift report, staple credit slips, and submit it to the manager.**  
  * *Verify that all transactions are accurate and properly recorded.*  
  * *Inform the MOD of any discrepancies or special notes before leaving.*

### ⭐**Enhancements: Activities & Demonstrations**

* **Menu Breakdown Session:** Work with your trainer to fully break down and discuss menu highlights, best-sellers, and common modifications.  
* **Mock Guest Interaction Drill:** Role-play guest scenarios, including handling food allergies, complaints, and last-minute modifications.  
* **Cross-Training in Expo:** Spend time shadowing expo staff to understand order accuracy, plating, and food running procedures.

---

* **Know the Details:** Guests expect servers to be menu experts—be ready with clear descriptions and recommendations.  
* **Stay Calm Under Pressure:** Difficult situations happen. The best servers resolve issues with professionalism and efficiency.  
* **Build Trust with Guests:** Honesty and confidence go a long way. If you don’t know something, find the answer quickly.

---

## ![white\_check\_mark emoji][image29] **Trainer to Trainee: Check for Understanding**

* **Comprehension Check:** "How do you properly communicate a guest’s dietary restriction to the kitchen?"  
* **Scenario:** "A guest sends back a dish because it’s not what they expected. How do you handle this professionally?"

---

## ![dart emoji][image30] **Performance Targets**

* **Menu Mastery:** Demonstrate full knowledge of food and beverage items, including ingredients and dietary accommodations.  
* **Guest Problem-Solving:** Show confidence in handling service challenges with professionalism and efficiency.  
* **Cross-Training Awareness:** Understand the expo role and how it impacts smooth service.  
* **Closing Responsibilities:** Complete shift reports and checklist tasks without errors.

## 4️⃣Server Day 4: Guest Relations & Closing Procedures

---

### 🎯**Objectives**

## **What You'll Learn Today**

Today, you will focus on enhancing guest interactions, providing personalized service, and handling difficult situations with professionalism. You'll also complete your final cross-training session and gain hands-on experience with closing responsibilities. This training will prepare you to deliver exceptional hospitality, exceed guest expectations, and confidently handle any service challenges.

## **Why It's Important**

Guest service extends beyond taking orders—it's about building relationships, reading guest needs, and creating memorable dining experiences. Handling challenges professionally and working as a team to close efficiently ensures smooth operations and prepares you for independent shifts.

# 

### ✅**Training Checklist & Key Points**

| Cross-Training Session \#2 Objective: Complete your final Expo cross-training session to reinforce understanding of BOH/FOH operations. |
| ----- |

*This segment recaps key topics from earlier sessions, ensuring you understand the core responsibilities and skills for the expo role. It includes task reviews, Q\&A, and extra practice as needed.*

## **Your Tasks:**

* **Expo Station Setup Recap:** Review the process of setting up the expo station. Discuss any challenges encountered during the previous session and strategies to overcome them.  
* **Expediting Food Orders Recap:** Reflect on the experience of expediting food orders from the previous session. Discuss successes and areas for improvement.  
* **Understanding the Expo Role Recap:** Revisit the responsibilities of the expo position, with a focus on effective coordination and communication with the kitchen staff.  
* **Station Cleanliness Recap:** Review the importance of maintaining a clean and organized station. Discuss techniques and best practices for keeping the station sanitary.  
* **Order Coordination Recap:** Reflect on coordinating orders between the kitchen and serving staff. Identify any difficulties and discuss effective solutions.  
* **Quality Control Recap:** Review the importance of quality control. Discuss any instances where quality control was challenging and how to address them.  
* **Timing and Efficiency Recap:** Reflect on managing the timing of orders, especially during peak service times. Discuss any timing issues and how to improve overall efficiency.  
* **Communication Skills Recap:** Review the importance of clear and concise communication. Discuss any communication issues faced and how to improve them.  
* **Handling Special Requests Recap:** Reflect on handling special requests and modifications. Discuss any challenges encountered and how to improve accuracy.  
* **Final Review and Wrap-Up:** Summarize the key learnings from the session. Discuss any remaining questions or concerns with your trainer or the Manager on Duty (MOD).

#### 

| Once you've completed this final Cross-Training Expo Session, please find your assigned trainer for the remainder of your shift. |
| :---- |

## **Step 2: Mid-Shift Tasks**

* **Begin working independently while your trainer oversees your progress.**  
  * *Handle multiple tables, manage guest requests, and practice upselling.*  
  * *Ask for feedback on your pacing, efficiency, and guest interactions.*

## **Step 3: Exceeding Guest Expectations**

* **Learn techniques for personalizing service and anticipating guest needs.**  
  * *Offer proactive service—suggest refills, provide extra napkins, and check on tables before being asked.*  
  * *Use guest names (when appropriate) and remember preferences to build rapport.*

## **Step 4: Active Listening & Communication Skills**

* **Improve both verbal and non-verbal communication to engage with guests effectively.**  
  * *Learn techniques for reading guest cues and adapting your approach.*  
  * *Practice speaking clearly, confidently, and professionally at all times.*

## **Step 5: Handling Complaints & Difficult Situations**

* **Follow a step-by-step process for resolving guest complaints calmly and professionally.**  
  * *Practice de-escalation techniques for challenging interactions.*  
  * *Understand when and how to involve management to resolve larger concerns.*

## **Step 6: Special Occasions & Large Party Service**

* **Learn best practices for handling large reservations and special event tables.**  
  * *Review the* [six-item rule](https://app.trainual.com/f9e05a9e-ccec-463e-beae-d1c5489f4c52/steps/20329588/consume) *for kitchen and bar service efficiency.*  
  * *Understand how to balance timing, guest expectations, and team coordination.*

## **Step 7: Cultural Sensitivity & Diversity**

* **Understand the importance of providing inclusive service to all guests.**  
  * *Learn how to approach different cultural dining norms with respect and awareness.*  
  * *Ensure guests feel valued, no matter their background or preferences.*

## **Step 8: Role-Playing Exercises**

* **Practice handling various guest scenarios, from complaints to special celebrations.**  
  * *Simulate interactions where you must resolve guest dissatisfaction.*  
  * *Learn how to turn a negative experience into a positive outcome.*

## **Step 9: Review of Company Policies**

* **Revisit key policies related to guest interactions and problem-solving.**  
  * *Ensure you understand the proper procedures for refunds, comps, and guest accommodations.*  
  * *Review policies on service refusals, underage guests, and alcohol regulations.*

## **Step 10: Closing Checklist**

* **Complete the closing checklist using the Jolt app with your trainer.**  
  * *Ensure all service stations are cleaned, stocked, and organized for the next shift.*  
  * *Understand how a well-executed closing shift sets up success for the following day.*

## **Step 11: Closing Reports**

* **Run the server shift report, attach credit slips, and submit it to management.**  
  * *Confirm all transactions are accurate before clocking out.*  
  * *Address any outstanding issues or special notes with the MOD before leaving.*

### ⭐**Enhancements: Activities & Demonstrations**

## **![key emoji][image34] Polishing Communication, Table Management & Recovery**

* **Table-Side Hospitality Exercise:** Engage in a simulated dining experience where you personalize guest service.  
* **De-Escalation Role-Play:** Work through guest complaints and challenging service situations to practice professional conflict resolution.  
* **Large Party Coordination Drill:** Simulate serving a large party, balancing service timing and multiple guest needs.

---

## ![bulb emoji][image35] **Pro Tips**

* **Personalization Matters:** A small touch—like remembering a guest's name or order preference—can make a lasting impression.  
* **Stay Professional, Even in Difficult Situations:** Guests may become frustrated, but how you handle it defines their experience.  
* **Efficiency is Key:** Keeping a smooth service flow, even with large parties, showcases your ability to manage high-volume service.

---

* **Handling Large Parties:** Demonstrate the ability to manage multiple guests while maintaining a smooth service flow.  
* **Closing Efficiency:** Execute closing procedures accurately, ensuring a clean and well-prepared service area.

## 4️⃣Server Day 5: Solo Service Readiness & Final Training Wrap-Up

---

### 🎯**Objectives**

## **What You'll Learn Today**

Today marks the final day of your structured training\! The focus will be on demonstrating your ability to work independently, handling full table service, and reinforcing all skills learned throughout the program. You will also complete a final service run-through, take on advanced POS transactions, and prepare for your mock service examination.

## **Why It’s Important**

A successful server is efficient, adaptable, and confident in managing multiple tables. Today’s training ensures you are ready for independent shifts by refining time management, service efficiency, and problem-solving skills.

### ✅**Training Checklist & Key Points**

## **![clipboard emoji][image41] Training Checklist & Key Points**

## **Step 1: Comprehensive Service Run-Through**

* **Perform a full-service simulation from greeting to payment.**  
  * *Manage multiple tables while ensuring smooth service flow.*  
  * *Demonstrate strong guest interactions and pacing strategies.*

## **Step 2: Advanced POS Scenarios**

* **Handle complex orders, voids, comps, and discounts in the POS system.**  
  * *Practice processing split checks, large party tabs, and troubleshooting payment issues.*  
  * *Ensure all transactions are recorded accurately and efficiently.*

## **Step 3: Team Service Exercises**

* **Work alongside fellow servers to practice effective hand-offs and team coordination.**  
  * *Understand how to assist with other tables while maintaining primary responsibilities.*  
  * *Demonstrate smooth communication between FOH and BOH.*

## **Step 4: Personal Development Plan**

* **Set goals for continued growth and improvement.**  
  * *Review feedback from trainers and managers to identify strengths and areas for improvement.*  
  * *Understand ongoing training opportunities within Three Points Hospitality.*

## **Step 5: Shadowing an Experienced Server**

* **Observe an experienced server during a full-service shift.**  
  * *Ask final questions regarding service flow, handling difficult guests, and efficiency techniques.*  
  * *Take notes on how experienced staff manage high-volume service.*

## **Step 6: Final Order Execution Drill**

* **Take orders from real guests under the supervision of your trainer.**  
  * *Ensure proper pacing, accuracy, and confidence in guest interactions.*  
  * *Receive feedback on your final training shift before transitioning to full independence.*

## **Step 7: End-of-Shift Responsibilities**

* **Complete your shift wrap-up by finalizing reports and assisting with closing duties.**  
  * *Ensure all tables are reset, side work is completed, and closing procedures are followed.*  
  * *Submit shift reports and confirm all outstanding tasks before clocking out.*

### ⭐**Enhancements: Activities & Demonstrations**

## **![key emoji][image42] Executing Full Service with Confidence & Speed**

* **Service Speed & Accuracy Test:** Execute multiple tables efficiently while maintaining attention to guest needs.  
* **POS Transaction Challenge:** Practice handling complex transactions and payment issues with trainer supervision.  
* **Guest Interaction Final Drill:** Role-play high-stress guest interactions, such as handling complaints and upselling opportunities.

---

## ![bulb emoji][image43] **Pro Tips**

* **Pace Yourself:** A steady, controlled approach to service leads to better guest experiences and fewer mistakes.  
* **Multi-Task Effectively:** Balance multiple tables by prioritizing tasks and staying ahead of guest needs.  
* **Stay Engaged:** Friendly, professional interactions make a lasting impression on guests and impact tips.

---

## ![white\_check\_mark emoji][image44] **Trainer to Trainee: Check for Understanding**

* **Comprehension Check:** "What are three best practices for managing multiple tables efficiently?"  
* **Scenario:** "A guest has an issue with their check and is disputing a charge. How do you resolve this professionally?"

---

## ![dart emoji][image45] **Performance Targets**

* **Service Independence:** Successfully complete full table service without trainer intervention.  
* **Transaction Mastery:** Execute all types of transactions in the POS system with confidence.  
* **Time Management & Efficiency:** Manage multiple tables while maintaining exceptional guest service.  
* **Closing Readiness:** Demonstrate knowledge of closing duties and complete shift wrap-up without errors.

### ✅**Server Final Examination & Mock Service**

### **Final Examination & Mock Service Scheduling**

To complete your training, you must contact your Trainer and/or Manager on Duty (MOD) to schedule your final examinations. These are separate from the knowledge checks in Trainual and must be completed **within 10 days** of starting training.

Once you have finished all 5 days of training, **submit the form** below to notify management that you are ready to take your final examinations or need an extra day or two of studying.

After passing, you will be scheduled for an **EIT (Employee in Training) Mock Service Examination**, where you will demonstrate everything you've learned by following the full Steps of Service. You will receive a grading form before your Mock Service so you know exactly what to focus on to succeed.

# **DOCUMENT:**

# **(ENGAGED & REVISED) SERVER C.O.R.E. TRAINING PROGRAM**

# **Day 1: Laying the Groundwork**

# **![dart emoji][image56] Objectives**

## **What You'll Learn Today**

Welcome to your first day of training\! You'll begin with an introduction to the company, expectations of the server role, and foundational service skills. You'll review key policies, familiarize yourself with the restaurant layout, and observe how experienced servers interact with guests.

## **Why It's Important**

A great server is attentive, efficient, and knowledgeable. Learning service flow, guest interaction skills, and menu knowledge will help you deliver exceptional experiences from day one. Today's foundation will set you up for high-volume service and advanced techniques in the days ahead.

# ![clipboard emoji][image57] **Training Checklist & Key Points**

## **Step 1: Welcome & Introduction**

* **Meet with the Manager on Duty (MOD) to submit paperwork, clock in, and get acquainted with the venue.**  
  * *Your trainer will introduce you to the 5-day training program, explaining on-site and off-site expectations.*  
  * *Use this time to ask any questions about your training schedule or expectations.*

## **Step 2: Company Policies & Procedures**

* **Review the company mission, vision, and core values.**  
  * *Discuss attendance policies, uniform expectations, and the structure of the training program.*  
  * *Understanding our core values ensures consistency and professionalism in service.*

## **Step 3: Uniforms, Personal Hygiene, Health & Safety Regulations, Serving Alcohol**

* **Learn the importance of maintaining a professional appearance and following hygiene standards.**  
  * *Review the Alcohol Agreement & Training requirements for responsible service.*  
  * *Cleanliness and personal hygiene are key to guest trust and upholding safety standards.*

## **Step 4: Payrolls, Tips & Scheduling**

* **Your trainer will go over payroll systems, tip distribution, and scheduling tools (7Shifts & Branch).**  
  * *Understand how you're scheduled, how you're paid, and ways to optimize earnings.*  
  * *A well-prepared server can focus on service without payroll concerns.*

## **Step 5: The Grand Tour**

* **Your trainer will give you a tour of the venue, including:**  
  * *Front-of-House (FOH): Dining sections, POS stations, host stand, bar, restrooms, entryways, and emergency exits.*  
  * *Back-of-House (BOH): Service stations, kitchen areas, dish pit, walk-in coolers/freezers, ice machines, storage areas, and employee break areas.*  
  * *Knowing your surroundings ensures efficiency during shifts.*

## **Step 6: Opening Checklist**

* **Review and complete the opening checklist using the Jolt app with your trainer.**  
  * *Learn proper setup procedures for expo stations and drink stations.*  
  * *Observing and following the opening process prepares you for independent execution.*

## **Step 7: Basic Serving Skills**

* **Learn proper greeting techniques, table approach, and tray/plate carrying.**  
  * *Understand "full hands in, full hands out" for efficiency.*  
  * *Practice inputting orders into the Toast POS system.*

## **Step 8: Table Setting, Numbers & Seating**

* **Observe and practice setting tables to restaurant standards.**  
  * *Learn table and seat numbers to ensure precise service.*  
  * *Accurate knowledge of seating arrangements improves order efficiency.*

## **Step 9: Proper Service Etiquette in Clearing Tables**

* **Discuss polite table-clearing etiquette and the correct time to clear items.**  
  * *Review guest interactions regarding when to remove plates.*  
  * *A clean and reset table enhances the guest experience.*

## **Step 10: Guest Interaction**

* **Observe how your trainer greets tables and interacts with guests.**  
  * *Ensure your introduction is warm, unique, and welcoming.*  
  * *Learn how to mention specials and allow guests time to review menus.*

## **Step 11: Service Observation**

* **Watch your trainer manage their tables to understand pacing, guest requests, and upselling techniques.**  
  * *Take note of how drink refills and check-ins are timed to enhance service flow.*  
  * *Learning from an experienced server provides valuable insights into efficiency.*

## **Step 12: POS System Introduction**

* **Observe and learn the Toast POS system, including:**  
  * *Inputting orders, separating by seat number, and processing payments.*  
  * *Splitting checks, adjusting tips, and handling voids or modifications.*  
  * *Efficiency in the POS system ensures smooth service and accurate billing.*

## **Step 13: Menu Familiarization**

* **Review the food menu, including specials, core dishes, and drink options.**  
  * *Learn suggested food and beverage pairings.*  
  * *Understanding menu items allows for confident guest recommendations.*

## **Step 14: Common Modifications**

* **Discuss frequent menu modifications, including dietary restrictions and allergies.**  
  * *Review gluten-free, vegetarian, and vegan menu items.*  
  * *Knowing common modifications helps accommodate guest needs smoothly.*

## **Step 15: End-of-Shift Recap**

* **Review and reinforce the key concepts from the day's training.**  
  * *Reflect on what was learned and identify areas for further practice.*  
  * *Ensure readiness for Day 2 training by reviewing key points.*

# *![pushpin emoji][image58]* **Enhancements: Activities & Demonstrations**

## **![key emoji][image59] Building the Basics of Service & Flow**

* **Venue Walkthrough:** Trainer-led tour focusing on guest areas, service stations, and key storage locations.  
* **Service Observation Drill:** Watch a senior server complete an entire table service cycle and take notes.  
* **Table Number Memory Challenge:** Quiz yourself on seat numbers and table layout for efficient service.

## ![bulb emoji][image60] **Pro Tips**

* **Stay Observant:** Pay close attention to experienced servers and their guest interactions.  
* **Ask Questions:** Use training time to clarify anything about menu items, service expectations, or venue logistics.  
* **First Impressions Matter:** A confident greeting sets the tone for great service—practice your table approach.

## ![white\_check\_mark emoji][image61] **Trainer to Trainee: Check for Understanding**

* **Comprehension Check:** "What are the key responsibilities of a server beyond taking orders?"  
* **Scenario:** "A guest has a food allergy and asks about menu options. How do you ensure their safety?"

## ![dart emoji][image62] **Performance Targets**

* **Company Knowledge:** Demonstrate understanding of venue expectations, policies, and service standards.  
* **Venue Familiarity:** Identify key locations in FOH and BOH to ensure smooth workflow.  
* **Basic Service Knowledge:** Display confidence in table numbers, basic serving techniques, and POS navigation.  
* **Guest Interaction Readiness:** Observe and understand best practices in greeting and table management.

By the end of Day 1, you should feel comfortable navigating the restaurant, familiar with company policies, and confident in observing service interactions. Today's training focused on laying the foundation for efficiency, guest engagement, and professionalism in service.

# **Day 2: Strengthening Service Skills**

# **![dart emoji][image66] Objectives**

## **What You'll Learn Today**

Building on what you learned in Day 1, today's focus is on deepening your menu knowledge, refining service techniques, and introducing upselling strategies. You'll also practice taking and inputting orders while learning how to manage multiple tables efficiently.

## **Why It's Important**

Guests rely on servers to provide accurate information about food, beverages, and modifications. Your confidence in the menu and service flow will directly impact guest satisfaction. Practicing today's skills will prepare you to handle guest requests, upsell effectively, and work efficiently during peak hours.

# ![clipboard emoji][image67] **Training Checklist & Key Points**

## **Step 1: Opening Checklist**

* **Arrive promptly, clock in, and complete the opening checklist using the Jolt app.**  
  * *Work with your trainer to ensure all FOH areas are guest-ready.*  
  * *A properly set-up dining area leads to a smooth service from the start.*

## **Step 2: Service Sequence Review**

* **Walk through the entire service flow—from greeting to farewell.**  
  * *Practice approaching a table, taking orders, and presenting checks.*  
  * *Consistency in service ensures a seamless guest experience.*

## **Step 3: Trainer Support & Guest Engagement**

* **Begin practicing order-taking, answering guest questions, and handling special requests.**  
  * *Learn how to manage multiple tables efficiently.*  
  * *Guest interaction and attentiveness directly impact guest satisfaction and tips.*

## **Step 4: Menu Training**

* **Review key menu items, including ingredients and preparation methods.**  
  * *Discuss guest favorites, seasonal specials, and high-margin items.*  
  * *A deep understanding of the menu makes you a trusted resource for guests.*

## **Step 5: Review of Food Allergies**

* **Learn about common food allergies (nuts, dairy, gluten, shellfish, soy, eggs).**  
  * *Understand the severity of allergic reactions and how to prevent cross-contamination.*  
  * *Guest safety is the top priority when handling dietary restrictions.*

## **Step 6: Review of Dietary Options**

* **Understand different dietary restrictions (vegetarian, vegan, gluten-free, keto).**  
  * *Familiarize yourself with menu options that cater to these needs.*  
  * *Being prepared with substitutions makes service smoother and more enjoyable for guests.*

## **Step 7: Beverage Service Basics**

* **Review liquor, beer, wine, and signature cocktails.**  
  * *Understand responsible alcohol service and ID verification.*  
  * *Proper knowledge of beverages allows you to make great pairing suggestions.*

## **Step 8: Table Management & Pacing**

* **Learn techniques for managing multiple tables efficiently.**  
  * *Prioritize tasks, maintain table awareness, and stay organized.*  
  * *Balancing multiple guests ensures that service runs smoothly even during busy hours.*

## **Step 9: Teamwork & Communication**

* **Understand the importance of pre-shift meetings and ongoing team communication.**  
  * *Work closely with kitchen staff, bartenders, and other servers.*  
  * *Strong communication leads to fewer mistakes and better guest service.*

## **Step 10: Upselling Techniques**

* **Learn strategies for increasing check averages through effective suggestions.**  
  * *Practice upselling with a trainer using real menu scenarios.*  
  * *Recommending enhancements improves the guest experience and increases sales.*

## **Step 11: Service Skills Practice**

* **Continue working with your trainer to take orders, run food, and interact with guests.**  
  * *Refine service etiquette, pacing, and handling guest needs.*  
  * *Hands-on practice builds confidence in real-world service scenarios.*

## **Step 12: Toast POS Practice**

* **Practice advanced POS functions (split checks, managing tabs, special requests).**  
  * *Troubleshoot common POS issues with your trainer.*  
  * *Mastering the POS system ensures efficiency and accuracy during peak service.*

## **Step 13: End-of-Shift Recap**

* **Review today's key concepts with your trainer.**  
  * *Discuss what went well and areas for improvement.*  
  * *Reflecting on service helps refine skills for the next shift.*

# *![pushpin emoji][image68]* **Enhancements: Activities & Demonstrations**

## **![key emoji][image69] Strengthening Service Steps & Menu Mastery**

* **Table-Side Interaction Drill:** Roleplay taking orders with guests who have specific dietary needs or modifications.  
* **Upselling Scenarios:** Practice recommending add-ons, beverage pairings, and dessert options in live role-play.  
* **Service Pacing Test:** Work with a trainer to practice handling multiple tables, tracking order timing, and ensuring proper course pacing.

### ![bulb emoji][image70] **Pro Tips**

* **Anticipate Needs:** Always offer drink refills, extra napkins, or condiments before the guest asks.  
* **Read Your Guests:** Some guests prefer quick service, while others enjoy conversation—adjust your approach accordingly.  
* **Confidence is Key:** Guests trust servers who confidently describe dishes and recommend pairings.

### ![white\_check\_mark emoji][image71] **Trainer to Trainee: Check for Understanding**

* **Comprehension Check:** "What is the best way to handle a guest with a severe food allergy?"  
* **Scenario:** "A guest is hesitant about ordering a specific dish. How do you guide them to a confident decision?"

### ![dart emoji][image72] **Performance Targets**

* **Menu Knowledge:** Demonstrate understanding of food ingredients, preparation, and dietary accommodations.  
* **Service Pacing:** Successfully manage multiple tables while maintaining efficiency.  
* **Upselling Skills:** Confidently recommend add-ons and pairings to enhance guest experience.  
* **POS Mastery:** Input orders and process checks without errors or delays.

# **Day 3: Mastering Menu Knowledge & Complex Service Scenarios**

# **![dart emoji][image76] Objectives**

## **What You'll Learn Today**

Today’s focus is on **mastering menu knowledge, handling special requests, and navigating complex service scenarios.** You'll engage in cross-training to understand how different roles contribute to smooth service. Additionally, you'll sharpen your ability to **make recommendations, upsell effectively, and problem-solve guest concerns.**

## **Why It’s Important**

A knowledgeable server **anticipates guest needs, confidently explains the menu, and provides solutions in real time.** Understanding menu items, dietary accommodations, and service strategies will enhance both the guest experience and your efficiency. By today’s end, you’ll be better equipped to **handle guest concerns and work seamlessly with the team.**

# **![clipboard emoji][image77] Training Checklist & Key Points**

## **Step 1: Independent Service Practice**

* **Begin working with your trainer while taking more responsibility for your tables.**  
  * *Manage tables with trainer oversight, while shadowing them for larger parties.*  
  * *Gain confidence in pacing service and prioritizing multiple guests.*

## **Step 2: Comprehensive Menu Training**

* **Deep dive into all menu items, including ingredients and preparation methods.**  
  * *Identify key allergens and dietary restrictions associated with each dish.*  
  * *Understand the origins of specialty dishes and how to describe them confidently.*

## **Step 3: Beverage Menu Deep Dive**

* **Review detailed knowledge of the wine list, beer selection, and signature cocktails.**  
  * *Discuss flavor profiles and ideal pairings with food menu items.*  
  * *Master basic beverage terminology to enhance guest recommendations.*

## **Step 4: Advanced Service Techniques**

* **Learn how to handle guest complaints and resolve service issues professionally.**  
  * *Practice maintaining composure and problem-solving under pressure.*  
  * *Understand how to offer solutions while following company policies.*

## **Step 5: Complex Service Scenarios**

* **Simulate handling difficult guest situations, including allergies, modifications, and high-stress environments.**  
  * *Role-play common service challenges and how to navigate them effectively.*  
  * *Learn how to involve management when necessary without escalating tension.*

## **Step 6: Handling Special Requests**

* **Discuss accommodating dietary restrictions and how to communicate modifications with the kitchen.**  
  * *Understand the difference between allergies and preferences to avoid miscommunication.*  
  * *Ensure cross-contamination prevention procedures are followed.*

## **Step 7: Cross-Training Expo Session \#1 (2 hours)**

* **Shadow the expo position to understand kitchen flow and order coordination.**  
  * *Work with kitchen staff to run food, check orders for accuracy, and ensure proper plating.*  
  * *Gain a deeper understanding of timing and communication between FOH and BOH.*

## **Step 8: Product Assessment Questionnaire**

* **Engage in a quiz with your trainer to test your knowledge of key menu items.**  
  * *Demonstrate your ability to describe dishes, allergens, and modifications confidently.*  
  * *Review guest FAQs and practice responding with clear, professional explanations.*

## **Step 9: Ending Shift Checklist**

* **Complete the mid/closing checklist using the Jolt app.**  
  * *Ensure tables are fully reset, service areas are stocked, and the restaurant is prepped for the next shift.*  
  * *Confirm that all tasks are completed before clocking out.*

## **Step 10: Closing Reports**

* **Run the server shift report, staple credit slips, and submit it to the manager.**  
  * *Verify that all transactions are accurate and properly recorded.*  
  * *Inform the MOD of any discrepancies or special notes before leaving.*

# *![pushpin emoji][image78]* **Enhancements: Activities & Demonstrations**

## **![key emoji][image79] Mastering Menu Confidence & Guest Customization**

* **Menu Breakdown Session:** Work with your trainer to fully break down and discuss menu highlights, best-sellers, and common modifications.  
* **Mock Guest Interaction Drill:** Role-play guest scenarios, including handling food allergies, complaints, and last-minute modifications.  
* **Cross-Training in Expo:** Spend time shadowing expo staff to understand order accuracy, plating, and food running procedures.

## ![bulb emoji][image80] **Pro Tips**

* **Know the Details:** Guests expect servers to be menu experts—be ready with clear descriptions and recommendations.  
* **Stay Calm Under Pressure:** Difficult situations happen. The best servers resolve issues with professionalism and efficiency.  
* **Build Trust with Guests:** Honesty and confidence go a long way. If you don’t know something, find the answer quickly.

## ![white\_check\_mark emoji][image81] **Trainer to Trainee: Check for Understanding**

* **Comprehension Check:** "How do you properly communicate a guest’s dietary restriction to the kitchen?"  
* **Scenario:** "A guest sends back a dish because it’s not what they expected. How do you handle this professionally?"

## ![dart emoji][image82] **Performance Targets**

* **Menu Mastery:** Demonstrate full knowledge of food and beverage items, including ingredients and dietary accommodations.  
* **Guest Problem-Solving:** Show confidence in handling service challenges with professionalism and efficiency.  
* **Cross-Training Awareness:** Understand the expo role and how it impacts smooth service.  
* **Closing Responsibilities:** Complete shift reports and checklist tasks without errors.

# **Day 4: Guest Relations & Closing Procedures**

# **![dart emoji][image86] Objectives**

## **What You'll Learn Today**

Today, you will focus on **enhancing guest interactions, providing personalized service, and handling difficult situations with professionalism.** You'll also complete your **final cross-training session** and gain hands-on experience with closing responsibilities. This training will prepare you to **deliver exceptional hospitality, exceed guest expectations, and confidently handle any service challenges.**

## **Why It's Important**

Guest service extends beyond taking orders—it's about **building relationships, reading guest needs, and creating memorable dining experiences.** Handling challenges professionally and **working as a team to close efficiently** ensures smooth operations and prepares you for independent shifts.

# ![clipboard emoji][image87] **Training Checklist & Key Points**

## **Step 1: Cross-Training Expo Session \#2 (2 hours)**

* **Complete your final cross-training session by working alongside the expo team.**  
  * *Review order accuracy, proper plating, and ticket management.*  
  * *Understand how communication between the kitchen and servers impacts food timing.*

## **Step 2: Mid-Shift Tasks**

* **Begin working independently while your trainer oversees your progress.**  
  * *Handle multiple tables, manage guest requests, and practice upselling.*  
  * *Ask for feedback on your pacing, efficiency, and guest interactions.*

## **Step 3: Exceeding Guest Expectations**

* **Learn techniques for personalizing service and anticipating guest needs.**  
  * *Offer proactive service—suggest refills, provide extra napkins, and check on tables before being asked.*  
  * *Use guest names (when appropriate) and remember preferences to build rapport.*

## **Step 4: Active Listening & Communication Skills**

* **Improve both verbal and non-verbal communication to engage with guests effectively.**  
  * *Learn techniques for reading guest cues and adapting your approach.*  
  * *Practice speaking clearly, confidently, and professionally at all times.*

## **Step 5: Handling Complaints & Difficult Situations**

* **Follow a step-by-step process for resolving guest complaints calmly and professionally.**  
  * *Practice de-escalation techniques for challenging interactions.*  
  * *Understand when and how to involve management to resolve larger concerns.*

## **Step 6: Special Occasions & Large Party Service**

* **Learn best practices for handling large reservations and special event tables.**  
  * *Review the six-item rule for kitchen and bar service efficiency.*  
  * *Understand how to balance timing, guest expectations, and team coordination.*

## **Step 7: Cultural Sensitivity & Diversity**

* **Understand the importance of providing inclusive service to all guests.**  
  * *Learn how to approach different cultural dining norms with respect and awareness.*  
  * *Ensure guests feel valued, no matter their background or preferences.*

## **Step 8: Role-Playing Exercises**

* **Practice handling various guest scenarios, from complaints to special celebrations.**  
  * *Simulate interactions where you must resolve guest dissatisfaction.*  
  * *Learn how to turn a negative experience into a positive outcome.*

## **Step 9: Review of Company Policies**

* **Revisit key policies related to guest interactions and problem-solving.**  
  * *Ensure you understand the proper procedures for refunds, comps, and guest accommodations.*  
  * *Review policies on service refusals, underage guests, and alcohol regulations.*

## **Step 10: Closing Checklist**

* **Complete the closing checklist using the Jolt app with your trainer.**  
  * *Ensure all service stations are cleaned, stocked, and organized for the next shift.*  
  * *Understand how a well-executed closing shift sets up success for the following day.*

## **Step 11: Closing Reports**

* **Run the server shift report, attach credit slips, and submit it to management.**  
  * *Confirm all transactions are accurate before clocking out.*  
  * *Address any outstanding issues or special notes with the MOD before leaving.*

# *![pushpin emoji][image88]* **Enhancements: Activities & Demonstrations**

## **![key emoji][image89] Polishing Communication, Table Management & Recovery**

* **Table-Side Hospitality Exercise:** Engage in a simulated dining experience where you personalize guest service.  
* **De-Escalation Role-Play:** Work through guest complaints and challenging service situations to practice professional conflict resolution.  
* **Large Party Coordination Drill:** Simulate serving a large party, balancing service timing and multiple guest needs.

## ![bulb emoji][image90] **Pro Tips**

* **Personalization Matters:** A small touch—like remembering a guest's name or order preference—can make a lasting impression.  
* **Stay Professional, Even in Difficult Situations:** Guests may become frustrated, but how you handle it defines their experience.  
* **Efficiency is Key:** Keeping a smooth service flow, even with large parties, showcases your ability to manage high-volume service.

## ![white\_check\_mark emoji][image91] **Trainer to Trainee: Check for Understanding**

* **Comprehension Check:** "What are three ways to exceed guest expectations during their dining experience?"  
* **Scenario:** "A guest is upset about a long wait time. How do you de-escalate the situation and provide a positive resolution?"

## ![dart emoji][image92] **Performance Targets**

* **Guest Service Mastery:** Show confidence in engaging with guests, upselling, and resolving concerns professionally.  
* **Handling Large Parties:** Demonstrate the ability to manage multiple guests while maintaining a smooth service flow.  
* **Closing Efficiency:** Execute closing procedures accurately, ensuring a clean and well-prepared service area.  
* **Policy Awareness:** Understand company policies regarding guest interactions, service refusals, and refunds.

# **Day 5: Solo Service Readiness & Final Training Wrap-Up**

# **![dart emoji][image96] Objectives**

## **What You'll Learn Today**

Today marks the final day of your structured training\! The focus will be on demonstrating your ability to work independently, handling full table service, and reinforcing all skills learned throughout the program. You will also complete a final service run-through, take on advanced POS transactions, and prepare for your mock service examination.

## **Why It’s Important**

A successful server is efficient, adaptable, and confident in managing multiple tables. Today’s training ensures you are ready for independent shifts by refining time management, service efficiency, and problem-solving skills.

# ![clipboard emoji][image97] **Training Checklist & Key Points**

## **Step 1: Comprehensive Service Run-Through**

* **Perform a full-service simulation from greeting to payment.**  
  * *Manage multiple tables while ensuring smooth service flow.*  
  * *Demonstrate strong guest interactions and pacing strategies.*

## **Step 2: Advanced POS Scenarios**

* **Handle complex orders, voids, comps, and discounts in the POS system.**  
  * *Practice processing split checks, large party tabs, and troubleshooting payment issues.*  
  * *Ensure all transactions are recorded accurately and efficiently.*

## **Step 3: Team Service Exercises**

* **Work alongside fellow servers to practice effective hand-offs and team coordination.**  
  * *Understand how to assist with other tables while maintaining primary responsibilities.*  
  * *Demonstrate smooth communication between FOH and BOH.*

## **Step 4: Personal Development Plan**

* **Set goals for continued growth and improvement.**  
  * *Review feedback from trainers and managers to identify strengths and areas for improvement.*  
  * *Understand ongoing training opportunities within Three Points Hospitality.*

## **Step 5: Shadowing an Experienced Server**

* **Observe an experienced server during a full-service shift.**  
  * *Ask final questions regarding service flow, handling difficult guests, and efficiency techniques.*  
  * *Take notes on how experienced staff manage high-volume service.*

## **Step 6: Final Order Execution Drill**

* **Take orders from real guests under the supervision of your trainer.**  
  * *Ensure proper pacing, accuracy, and confidence in guest interactions.*  
  * *Receive feedback on your final training shift before transitioning to full independence.*

## **Step 7: End-of-Shift Responsibilities**

* **Complete your shift wrap-up by finalizing reports and assisting with closing duties.**  
  * *Ensure all tables are reset, side work is completed, and closing procedures are followed.*  
  * *Submit shift reports and confirm all outstanding tasks before clocking out.*

# *![pushpin emoji][image98]* **Enhancements: Activities & Demonstrations**

## **![key emoji][image99] Executing Full Service with Confidence & Speed**

* **Service Speed & Accuracy Test:** Execute multiple tables efficiently while maintaining attention to guest needs.  
* **POS Transaction Challenge:** Practice handling complex transactions and payment issues with trainer supervision.  
* **Guest Interaction Final Drill:** Role-play high-stress guest interactions, such as handling complaints and upselling opportunities.

## ![bulb emoji][image100] **Pro Tips**

* **Pace Yourself:** A steady, controlled approach to service leads to better guest experiences and fewer mistakes.  
* **Multi-Task Effectively:** Balance multiple tables by prioritizing tasks and staying ahead of guest needs.  
* **Stay Engaged:** Friendly, professional interactions make a lasting impression on guests and impact tips.

## ![white\_check\_mark emoji][image101] **Trainer to Trainee: Check for Understanding**

* **Comprehension Check:** "What are three best practices for managing multiple tables efficiently?"  
* **Scenario:** "A guest has an issue with their check and is disputing a charge. How do you resolve this professionally?"

## ![dart emoji][image102] **Performance Targets**

* **Service Independence:** Successfully complete full table service without trainer intervention.  
* **Transaction Mastery:** Execute all types of transactions in the POS system with confidence.  
* **Time Management & Efficiency:** Manage multiple tables while maintaining exceptional guest service.  
* **Closing Readiness:** Demonstrate knowledge of closing duties and complete shift wrap-up without errors.

# **![checkered\_flag emoji][image106] Final Steps: Server Final Examination & Mock Service**

## 

## **Final Examination & Mock Service Scheduling**

To complete your training, you must **schedule your final examinations** with your Trainer or Manager on Duty (MOD). This test evaluates your knowledge of:

* **The Server Manual**  
* **Responsible Alcohol Service**  
* **Full Menu Knowledge (Including Allergens & Modifications)**  
* **POS System Mastery**  
* **Guest Service Scenarios**

Once your written test is complete, you will be scheduled for an **Employee in Training (EIT) Mock Service Examination,** where you will **demonstrate your ability to handle full-service tables independently.** You will receive a grading form in advance so you know exactly what to focus on.

This is the final step before officially becoming a fully trained server\!

## ![pushpin emoji][image107] **Final Checklist Before Your Mock Service**

* **Review all service steps, menu items, and policies before your final exam.**  
* **Continue practicing POS functions and handling guest interactions confidently.**  
* **Ask managers or trainers any last questions before taking on solo shifts.**

## **![rocket emoji][image108] Congratulations on Completing the Server C.O.R.E. Training Program\!**

You are now fully equipped to provide exceptional service, manage multiple tables efficiently, and contribute to a great guest experience. The skills you’ve learned in this program will serve as the foundation for your success as a server at Three Points Hospitality.

**Now, it’s time to step into your role and shine. Best of luck on your final assessments—see you on the floor\!**

## 1️⃣OLD: Day 1 Training – Orientation Phase

---

### 💡**Objective & Overview**

### **![mega emoji][image109] Welcome to Day 1 of Training\!**

As you start your journey as a server, this training checklist will guide you through the day’s key tasks. It’s designed to help you understand the basics of the server role, familiarize yourself with the restaurant’s environment, and begin developing the skills you’ll need to succeed. Each item on the checklist represents a critical step in your training, ensuring that by the end of the day, you have a solid foundation to build upon. Take your time with each task, ask questions when needed, and use this opportunity to absorb as much knowledge as possible. Let’s make the most of today’s training\!

---

![pushpin emoji][image110] **Objective:** Introduction to Company, Overview of the Server Role, & Initial Hands-On Training

![bulb emoji][image111]**Spotlight:** Welcome to your first day of training\! Start by locating the manager on duty to submit any paperwork, clock in, and get acquainted with your new surroundings. The manager will then introduce you to your trainer. Together, you'll tour different areas of the restaurant. During this orientation, you'll review the employee handbook, server handbook, and familiarize yourself with the venue's food and drink menus {location based}. Today you'll focus on understanding our company culture, basic serving techniques, and observing experienced servers in action.

### 👋**Welcome & Introduction**

Arrive promptly and find the manager on duty to submit your paperwork, clock in, and get acclimated. The manager will introduce you to your trainer and co-workers for the shift. Additional location-specific training will be tailored based on venue requirements. Any relatable experience you have will be factored into the pace of your training process

***{Reading} Employee Handbook Overview***

*This will be brief as you should have already read through, completed, and taken the test at the end.*

### 📜**Company Policies and Procedures**

Review the Employee Handbook; discuss our mission, vision, and core beliefs; cover employment policies, attendance requirements, and uniform standards; introduce our Golden Rules and Service Promise.

***{Reading} Employee Handbook Overview***

*This will be brief as you should have already read through, completed, and taken the test at the end.*

### 📄**Uniforms, Personal Hygiene, Health & Safety Regulations, Serving Alcohol**

Understand the importance of personal appearance and hygiene practices, alcohol agreement & training

***{Reading} Employee Handbook Overview***

*This will be brief as you should have already read through, completed, and taken the test at the end.*

### 💵**Payrolls, Tips, and Scheduling**

The trainer will go over with you the payroll system, tip reduction %, CC & cash tips, Branch, & 7Shifts scheduling.

### ✈️**The Grand Tour**

### **Your trainer will give you a tour of the venue:**

**Front of House (FOH) –** Location of the bar(s), POS stations,, host stand (location-based), bathroom locations, entryways, emergency exits

**Back-of-House (BOH) –** Service station (coffee/tea/espresso machine, soda fountain), location of all walk-in coolers/freezers, employee lockers, ice machines, employee bathrooms {location base}, manager office, kitchen/dish pit, storage rooms and backup products, and back entryways {location-based}

### 📋**Opening Checklist**

With the trainer, go through the opening checklist using the Jolt app. Observe each step, especially when learning the expo and drink station

* Observing and learning the opening procedures ensures you’re prepared to set up the service areas correctly and efficiently.

### 🍽️**Basic Serving Skills**

Proper greeting and table approach; carrying trays and plates (full hands in, full hands out); taking orders and inputting them into the Toast Point of Sale (POS) System

* Mastering basic serving techniques is essential for providing consistent, high-quality service from the start.

### 🔢**Table Setting, Numbers & Seating**

Observe and practice setting tables to restaurant standards. Learn table and seat numbers to ensure efficient service

* Learning table numbers and proper seating arrangements ensures that service flows smoothly and efficiently.

### 🤗**Proper Service Etiquette in Clearing Tables**

Overview with your trainer discussing and understanding polite inquiries, time of clearing, and clearing techniques

* Understanding when and how to clear tables politely helps maintain a clean and inviting dining area for all guests.

### 👥**Guest Interaction**

Observe how your trainer greets tables. Ensure your introduction is warm, unique, and welcoming. Mention any specials and give guests time to review the menus

* Observing how experienced servers interact with guests will give you practical insights into making guests feel welcome and valued.

### 👀**Service Observation**

Watch your trainer handle their tables throughout the visit. Learn to pace courses, ask for new drinks, and use upselling techniques

* Watching your trainer manage tables provides a real-world example of how to pace service and handle guest needs effectively.

### 💻**POS System Introduction**

Observe and learn the POS system, including ringing in orders, separating by seat number, splitting items, payment processing, closing out checks, and adjusting tips

* Familiarizing yourself with the POS system is critical for efficient order processing and accurate billing.

### 📋**Menu Familiarization**

Overview of the food menu, including any seasonal specials; introduction to our extensive drink menu, including signature cocktails; basic food and drink pairing suggestions

* Knowing the menu inside and out allows you to make informed recommendations and answer guest questions confidently.

### 🔄**Common Modifications**

Discuss with your trainer common modifications for food or drink items. Review dietary restriction food items (Gluten Free, Vegetarian, Vegan)

* Understanding common food and drink modifications will help you accommodate guest preferences and dietary restrictions smoothly.

### ☑️**Shift Wrap-Up & Checklist**
